summary: SanDisk is one of the strongest US large-cap momentum names in 2026, with one of the best year-to-date gains among S&P 500 stocks.[1]

Moderna has posted a very strong 2026 rebound, indicating renewed positive momentum in the shares.[1]
Generac is among the year’s top-performing stocks, reflecting sustained positive price momentum.[1]
Corning has been a standout 2026 performer, supported by strong relative strength.[1]
Teradyne has delivered a large year-to-date gain and continues to screen as a momentum leader.[1]
Western Digital is one of 2026’s top-performing large-cap tech names.[1]
Comfort Systems USA has produced an outsized 2026 advance, signaling strong momentum.[1]
Seagate Technology is another major 2026 winner with strong relative strength.[1]
Ciena has been a strong 2026 performer, keeping it on the momentum watchlist.[1]
Marathon Petroleum is highlighted by Zacks as a June 2026 buy candidate with strong 12-week price momentum and favorable earnings growth expectations.[3]
Mercury General appears on Zacks’ June 2026 list, pairing positive recent price action with favorable quantitative momentum.[3]
Malibu Boats is on Zacks’ June 2026 buy list with positive 12-week price change and improving projected sales growth.[3]
Apple remains a large, liquid mega-cap with persistent institutional sponsorship and relative strength compared with weaker mega-cap peers.[1]
Amazon is a mega-cap liquidity leader that can be used as a fallback momentum name despite its weaker 2026 YTD showing in the cited data.[1]
Microsoft is a dominant large-cap name with deep liquidity and persistent market relevance, making it a high-quality fallback momentum candidate.[1]
Nvidia remains a leading large-cap AI stock with enduring investor demand and strong market momentum characteristics.
Meta is a liquid mega-cap that has continued to attract buyers on strong earnings power and capital returns.

Oracle has been a strong large-cap software name with recurring AI and cloud momentum.

JPMorgan Chase is a liquid large-cap financial leader with durable earnings power and strong relative stability.
Exxon Mobil is a mega-cap energy name that remains a defensive large-cap fallback with persistent liquidity and market relevance.
Palo Alto Networks is a large-cap cybersecurity leader that has continued to trade as a momentum stock.
Visa is a high-quality, highly liquid large-cap payments name that often screens well when investors favor durable momentum and earnings consistency.
